title: Doctor Module Registry
description: The canonical, static list of Doctor Module Registry descriptors — Phase 3/WHIMS Batch WPI-3 (docs/50-PHASE-3-TECHNICAL-PLAN.md §7.1/§19, ADR-020, docs/53-PHASE-3-IMPLEMENTATION-RULES.md) shipped empty; Batch WPI-4 (docs/50 §7.3/§7.4/§19) added this registry's first real entry, patient_roster; Batch WPI-5 (docs/50 §8/§19) added this registry's second entry, appointments; Batch WPI-7 (docs/50 §10/§19) added this registry's third entry, inventory; Batch WPI-8 (docs/50 §11/§19) added this registry's fourth entry, pillfill_orders; Batch WPI-9 (docs/50 §12/§19) added this registry's fifth entry, analytics; Batch WPI-10 (docs/55-WPI-10-AI-ASSISTANT-ARCHITECTURE-FREEZE.md §13, ADR-021/022/023) adds this version's sixth entry, ai_assistant. Phase 3/WHIMS's Pillar 2 (docs/49 §4) — the platform's only mechanism for expressing which doctor-facing capabilities exist at all (availability), structurally parallel to shared/constants/module-registry.json's own 'availability, not enablement' framing, applied to a separate registry for a separate identity type (Doctor Identity, ADR-017) — never merged with the patient-facing registry (ADR-020). Per-doctor enablement is a separate, Sheet-backed concern (shared/schemas/doctor-module-state.schema.json). Batch WPI-10 registers 'ai_assistant' (docs/55 §13) — the platform's first AI-generated-content doctor capability (AIAssistantContext.gs/AIAssistantInteraction.gs), backed by a new, real, authenticated data_source route (get_ai_assistant_capabilities). Diverges from every prior entry in exactly one way, per ADR-023: this entry's own DoctorModuleState must remain absent (fail-closed, ADR-010) for every doctor by default, and enabling it is a deliberate, disclosed, per-doctor administrative decision — never a bulk/default rollout, unlike every prior entry's own lighter-touch enablement convention. Every other illustrative capability docs/50 §7.1 names (condition assignment, care-plan authoring, module/calculator/template enablement) remains unregistered — each is added as its own registry entry by whichever later, separately-approved WPI batch actually builds a real doctor-facing data_source route for it (docs/53 §4's 'a new registry entry, never new architecture'), the same discipline module-registry.json's and calculator-registry.json's own header comments already established for their own not-yet-built capabilities. apps-script/DoctorModuleRegistry.gs hand-ports this list into its own Foundation-prefixed array, the same convention module-registry.json's and calculator-registry.json's own consumers already use. See doctor-module-registry.md for the full field-by-field explanation and this batch's own disclosed scope decisions.
